summary: Review of changes addressing sourcery-ai[bot] feedback on CC-0036. Three issues were fixed: (1) `for i := range numKeys` replaced with `for i := 0; i < numKeys; i++` in both production and test code, (2) centralized `normalizedCredentialMaxActiveKeys()` helper created to enforce minimum floor of 3, (3) all three read-sites of MaxActiveKeys (secret creation, config rendering, CronJob env var) now use the normalized value. Two review pattern files were added documenting the identified anti-patterns for future reviews.

tests_checklist.item: Tests exist and pass (go test ./internal/controller/... — 0 failures)
Edge cases covered: MinActiveKeysFloor with MaxActiveKeys=1 generates 3 keys
Happy path covered: SecretAlreadyExists, CronJobScheduleUpdated, ConditionMessages
Key validity tested: base64url decoding produces 32 bytes
Key uniqueness tested: two consecutive generateFernetKey calls produce different keys
CronJob spec thoroughly asserted: labels, SA, init container, main container, env vars, volumes
PushSecret references asserted: selector, SecretStore, remote key
Config test covers [credential] section with key_repository and max_active_keys
Config test covers custom MaxActiveKeys=7 rendering correctly

code_quality_checklist.item: Follows existing 5-step sub-reconciler pattern from reconcile_fernet.go
All read-sites of MaxActiveKeys use normalized helper — no raw access
SPDX headers present on all files

security_checklist.item: RBAC follows least-privilege (get+patch on specific Secret)
Rotation script uses TLS for K8s API communication
No sensitive data in logs (print message is generic)

architecture_checklist.item: Solution mirrors existing Fernet pattern — minimal novelty
Single normalization helper prevents value inconsistency
Resource names derived from CR name (no hardcoding)

dry_yagni_checklist.item: No duplicated normalization logic — single helper function

fail_fast_checklist.item: Requeue after initial secret creation to confirm availability

defensive_checklist.item: All Kubernetes API errors checked and wrapped

suggested_improvements: The Fernet code (reconcile_fernet.go:172, :263 and reconcile_config.go:39) has the same normalization inconsistency that was just fixed for credential — `createFernetKeysSecret` applies `max(MaxActiveKeys, 3)` but the CronJob env var and config pass the raw value. Consider creating a matching `normalizedFernetMaxActiveKeys()` helper in a follow-up. Out of scope for CC-0036.

The Fernet code (reconcile_fernet.go:175) still uses `for i := range numKeys` (Go 1.22+ range-over-int). Go 1.25.7 is the minimum so it compiles fine, but it's now inconsistent with the credential code. Consider aligning in a follow-up.
